problem: Steve guesses randomly on a 15-question multiple choice test in which each problem has two choices, a correct one and an incorrect one. What is the probability that he gets at least half of the questions correct? Express your answer as a common fraction.

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of possible outcomes for Steve's guesses on the 15-question test?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many questions does Steve need to get correct in order to have at least half of them correct?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of getting exactly half of the questions correct?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many different combinations of correct and incorrect answers are there that result in Steve getting more than half of the questions correct?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you calculate the probability of getting more than half of the questions correct using the combinations calculated in the previous question?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other methods or strategies that Steve can use to improve his chances of getting at least half of the questions correct?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Is the probability of getting more than half of the questions correct the same as the probability of getting less than half correct? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b
